stem_explanations: no opening bid — fewer than about 11-12 points
opening values (11-16ish), diamonds — usually four-plus, no five-card major
no overcall and no takeout double of 1D
forcing one round: 6+ HCP, four-plus spades; bidding up-the-line, so denies four hearts
no sandwich action: nothing worth showing between the two bidders at this vulnerability
natural second suit: four-plus clubs with diamonds at least as long (usually 5-4 minors), 11-16 — no reverse, so not extra strength
still silent: no bid over either minor; discounts a decent five-card major with 10-13
simple preference: 6-9 with diamond tolerance and no game interest

X.shows: a takeout-shaped competitive double of the dying 2D: both majors, short diamonds, moderate values from a passed hand — asks partner to pick a suit
X.partner: picks a major at the two level (2S with four spades and ~7+, a jump to 3H with four hearts and ~10+) or passes for penalties with fat diamonds; the opening side can still run to 3C or compete to 3D

2H.shows: on the card, a sound live-auction overcall: about 11+ HCP with a decent five-card heart suit — partner will play you for that hand
2H.partner: raises to 3H with four trumps and about 9+, expecting the announced values; the opening side may push to 3C or 3D, and a heart stack behind you can double

P.shows: nothing to say: no shape or values worth a call, willing to defend 2D
P.partner: usually all pass and 2D plays; partner can still balance with a reopening double (~10+, short diamonds) or a five-card spade suit

stem_explanations: no opening bid — fewer than about 11-12 points
opening values, five-plus spades
no overcall and no double of 1S
6-10 HCP, at most two spades, no game interest yet
a passed hand with nothing to say over 1NT
natural rebid: five spades and four-plus clubs, minimum-range opener
still silent: no balancing action; discounts a decent six-card red suit with 10-14
club preference: content to play 2C, usually four clubs (sometimes three), still 6-10
balancing-style takeout double by a passed hand: both red suits, implied shortness in clubs and spades, roughly 9-11

3C.shows: a competitive raise of partner's chosen suit: 5-4 in the blacks with a minimum, outbidding the doubler's red suits — not invitational
3C.partner: passes throughout; the doubler's side frequently advances anyway to 3D or 3H in a red fit, and an opponent with club length and values can double

P.shows: no extra shape or strength: content to let the double stand and hear the advance
P.partner: the doubler's partner picks a red suit (2D or 2H) or converts to penalties by passing with club length, so 2C rarely plays undoubled; your side has no forced second call

2S.shows: on the card, a sixth spade: having opened 1S and rebid 2C, retreating to 2S over the double announces extra spade length — partner will believe six
2S.partner: holding at most two spades, passes and plays you for the six-card suit; the doubler's side can still advance to 3D or 3H, and an opponent with four good spades can double

stem_explanations: no opening bid as dealer
opening values, five-plus spades
no overcall and no double of 1S
2/1 game force: five-plus hearts, about 12+ HCP — the partnership is committed to game
nothing to say over 2H
spade rebid: often a sixth spade, or the default with no more descriptive call; no heart fit and no second suit shown yet
still nothing to say
natural 2NT: balanced-ish with at most two spades, typically 12-14 (stronger balanced hands are rare here) — still forcing
silent throughout

3H.shows: delayed three-card heart support: offers the 5-3 heart fit as a strain for game or slam — partner will count eight trumps
3H.partner: believing in the fit, raises to 4H with a sixth heart or a side doubleton, offers 3NT with a square minimum, and drives toward a heart slam with a maximum; a heart stack over dummy can double a 4H that gets pushed

3NT.shows: a direct sign-off try: minimum game-force values, no three-card heart support, no sixth spade, nothing left to describe
3NT.partner: passes with the normal 12-14; raises quantitatively to 4NT with about 16+, and carries on to a notrump slam with a genuine maximum

3C.shows: natural: four-plus clubs alongside the five spades, patterning out below 3NT and keeping every strain in play; still forcing
3C.partner: signs off in 3NT with the ordinary balanced 12-14; can rebid a sixth heart to reach 4H, raise clubs toward 5C with four-card support and extras, or drive to slam with a maximum

stem_explanations: no opening bid as dealer
no opening bid either — and no preempt shape
third-seat 1C: about 11-17 (occasionally shaded a point or two, occasionally a full 18-20), three-plus clubs
takeout double: opening values with club shortness and support for the majors and diamonds — or any 17+ hand planning to bid again

1D.shows: natural, four-plus diamonds; by not redoubling, roughly 6-9 — the hand's main feature named at the one level
1D.partner: opener usually passes or rebuys with 2C on a good six-card suit; the strong doubler comes again with 16+ — a major-suit bid or notrump, up to game with a huge hand

1S.shows: natural, four-plus spades; by not redoubling, roughly 6-9 — the cheapest major offered before the doubler's side finds it
1S.partner: opener raises with four spades or rebids a long club suit; a strong doubler bids on in hearts or notrump, and with good spades can double for penalties

P.shows: a weak responding hand with nothing convenient: denies 10+ (no redouble), a biddable suit worth naming, and a club raise
P.partner: the doubler's partner advances cheaply in a major, and a strong doubler raises — up to game with the 17+ type; occasionally 1C doubled is passed out and opener plays it doubled

2C.shows: a single raise: club support with about 6-9 in support — partner will read a sounder, chunkier raise, and the third-seat 1C may be only three cards
2C.partner: opener passes almost always, so the raise often buys the contract; a strong doubler still introduces a major or notrump over it, and 2C can be doubled when the advancer has trumps sitting well

stem_explanations: no opening bid as dealer
no opening bid — fewer than about 11-12 points
no light third-seat opening either — a weak hand
fourth-seat opening: sound values expecting a plus score, diamonds
no overcall and no double
natural 2C by a passed hand: five-plus clubs, near-maximum pass (about 9-11), not forcing to game
still nothing to say
minimum rebid: roughly 11-14 with five-plus diamonds and no five-card major; with four spades opener usually bids 2S here, so that holding is unlikely
silent to the end

2S.shows: a fresh suit at the two level: four spades in a hand still trying for the right partscore — partner will place you with 4 of them
2S.partner: with three-card spade support passes and plays what he believes is a 4-3 fit, raising to 3S with four and a maximum; he retreats to diamonds only with extreme shape, and an opponent with five good spades can double

P.shows: acceptance of 2D: tolerance for diamonds or no safer spot to offer
P.partner: the auction ends and 2D plays; the only variation is a late balance by the silent opponents into a major fit

3C.shows: rebiddable clubs: six-plus in a suit worth insisting on, no diamond fit, a sign-off from the passed hand
3C.partner: passes almost always and 3C plays; he returns to 3D only with six-plus diamonds, club shortness, and a maximum

stem_explanations: opening values, three-plus clubs, no five-card major
no overcall and no double of 1C; discounts a decent five-card major with 8-10
6+ points, four-plus diamonds, strength unlimited but usually a modest hand
overcall: roughly 8-16 with a decent five-plus spade suit

X.shows: a support double: exactly three-card diamond support, any strength within the opening — an accurate picture of the fit, promising nothing extra
X.partner: reading three trumps, competes to 2D or invites with 3D as his strength allows, or passes the double for penalties with spades stacked; the overcaller's side often raises to 2S anyway

P.shows: a minimum with no clear second call: not three diamonds, no dependable spade stopper, no sixth club — the decision is deferred
P.partner: acts again with about 8+ — repeating a five-card diamond suit, trying 1NT with spade values, or giving club preference; with less, the overcall often buys it, sometimes raised to 2S

1NT.shows: 12-14 balanced with a spade stopper: the standard notrump rebid in competition, claiming the overcalled suit is held
1NT.partner: passes with a modest hand or raises toward 3NT with about 13+; a strong hand over you can double 1NT, and the spade side may still compete to 2S

2C.shows: a free club rebid over the overcall: on the card this promises a sixth club in a minimum opener — partner will count six of them
2C.partner: mostly passes and 2C plays; he moves only with real extras, such as a 3D try on five-plus diamonds and 11+, while the overcaller's side often competes to 2S and a club stack behind you can double

stem_explanations: no opening bid — fewer than about 11-12 points
no opening bid either
opening: roughly 11-17 with three-plus diamonds, no five-card major
takeout double: opening values with diamond shortness and support for the unbid suits — or any 17+ hand
no redouble (so under about 10), no bid, no diamond raise — nearly always fewer than six points

2H.shows: a jump advance of the takeout double: four-plus hearts and about 9-11 in support points, invitational — partner will credit you with the full announced values
2H.partner: carries on to 4H with about 14+ or a shapely maximum, expecting your 9-11; opener can still compete to 3D with a long strong suit, and a heart stack can double a raised game

1H.shows: a minimum advance: hearts as the cheapest suit, anywhere from a near-bust to about 8 support points
1H.partner: passes with an ordinary balanced minimum; with about 14+ and four hearts he raises or invites — and this hand would accept an invitation — while opener may rebid 2D with six diamonds
